title: coelorachis khasiana
intro: Coelorachis khasiana is a rare and endangered grass species native to the Western Himalayas, specifically found in the Khasi and Jaintia hills of India. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an antioxidant. Coelorachis khasiana is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used to make a decoction or a powder for medicinal purposes.
